𝗩𝗜𝗗𝗘𝗢-𝗨𝗦𝗘 | Automating video editing through natural language commands processed by AI coding agents.  📈 Growing!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Automating video editing through natural language commands processed by AI coding agents.
📅 Created: Apr 2026 • Updated: May 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Cuts filler words and dead space
✅ Auto color grades segments
✅ Adds 30ms audio fades at cuts
✅ Burns in customizable subtitles
✅ Generates animation overlays
✅ Self-evaluates rendered output
✅ Persists session memory


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 7.4K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 1.1K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 3 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

video-use is an open-source Python tool that leverages AI coding agents to automate video editing tasks. It processes raw footage by cutting filler words, applying color grading, adding audio fades, burning in customizable subtitles, and generating animation overlays, all while self-evaluating the output.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/browser-use/video-use

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗧𝗬 | To provide an enhanced and visually rich terminal experience by enabling 3D graphics and GPU-accelerated rendering within the terminal interface.  🔥 Super Hot!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide an enhanced and visually rich terminal experience by enabling 3D graphics and GPU-accelerated rendering within the terminal interface.
📅 Created: Apr 2026 • Updated: May 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ GPU-backed text rendering
✅ Traditional 2D and new 3D mode
✅ Inline 3D objects
✅ Image support (Kitty Graphics Protocol)
✅ Customizable spinning rat cursor


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1.4K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 32 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 18 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Ratty is a GPU-accelerated terminal emulator built with Rust and Ratatui, offering traditional 2D text rendering alongside a novel 3D mode. It supports inline 3D objects and image rendering via the Kitty Graphics Protocol, providing a unique visual experience for terminal applications.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/orhun/ratty

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗟𝗔𝗬-𝗣𝗨𝗕𝗟𝗜𝗖 | Correcting the last typed word to the correct keyboard layout (e.g., Russian/English) with a double Shift press on Linux desktops, especially GNOME Wayland.  🔥 Super Hot!

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Correcting the last typed word to the correct keyboard layout (e.g., Russian/English) with a double Shift press on Linux desktops, especially GNOME Wayland.
📅 Created: May 2026 • Updated: May 2026

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Double Shift to fix the last word typed in the wrong layout
✅ Works directly in applications without clipboard use
✅ Supports GNOME Wayland via a Shell extension
✅ Experimental support for KDE/Plasma and X11 backends
✅ CLI for terminal text conversion
✅ Assisted typing after space
✅ Custom dictionary auto-substitution
✅ Window-specific layout binding (ptah_alexs mode)
✅ Local processing, no cloud or network requests


⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 52 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 3 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 6 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Lay is a Rust-based utility for Linux desktops, primarily GNOME Wayland, that corrects typing errors caused by incorrect keyboard layouts. It allows users to fix the last typed word by double-pressing the Shift key, seamlessly switching it to the intended layout without manual copy-pasting.

⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃⁃

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/radislabus-star/lay-public

𝗛𝗔𝗖𝗞𝗜𝗡𝗚𝗧𝗢𝗢𝗟 | To provide a unified and easy-to-use interface for accessing and managing a wide array of hacking and penetration testing tools for security professionals.  🌟 Rising Fast!

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a unified and easy-to-use interface for accessing and managing a wide array of hacking and penetration testing tools for security professionals.
📅 Created: Apr 2020 • Updated: May 2026

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ All-in-one hacking tool with 185+ tools
✅ Organized into 20 categories
✅ Supports web attacks, password attacks, wireless attacks, etc.
✅ Python 3.10+ based
✅ OS-aware menus (Linux/macOS)
✅ Search functionality by name, description, or keyword
✅ Tag filtering (osint, web, c2, cloud, mobile)
✅ Tool recommendation based on user intent
✅ Install status indicator for each tool
✅ Batch installation of tools
✅ Smart update mechanism for tools
✅ Docker support
✅ One-liner installation script


❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 74K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 8.3K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 96 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

HackingTool is a comprehensive, all-in-one Python-based toolkit designed for security researchers and penetration testers. It aggregates over 185 tools across 20 categories, offering functionalities for web attacks, password attacks, wireless attacks, and more, with features like smart search, tag filtering, and automated installation.

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/Z4nzu/hackingtool

𝗗𝗔𝗩𝗜𝗡𝗖𝗜-𝗥𝗘𝗦𝗢𝗟𝗩𝗘-𝗠𝗖𝗣 | To enable AI assistants to control and automate post-production workflows within DaVinci Resolve through natural language commands.  🌟 Rising Fast!

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To enable AI assistants to control and automate post-production workflows within DaVinci Resolve through natural language commands.
📅 Created: Mar 2025 • Updated: May 2026

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Complete coverage of DaVinci Resolve Scripting API
✅ Integration with AI assistants (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf)
✅ 30 context-efficient tools for post-production control
✅ Guarded kernel workflows for safe operations (probing, reporting, dry-runs)
✅ Support for timeline editing, media pool management, render/deliver, review annotations, color/grade, Fusion composition, conform/interchange, audio/Fairlight, and project lifecycle management.


❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 123 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 5 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

This Python-based MCP server provides comprehensive integration with the DaVinci Resolve Scripting API, enabling AI assistants to control post-production workflows. It offers 30 context-efficient tools for tasks like timeline editing, media management, rendering, and color grading.
